chapter_title: Gen. Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size & Weight
text:
(a) Except for the authority granted to a port-of-entry supervisor or inspector by Section 621.409 (Weighing of Loaded Vehicles by Port-of-entry Supervisors, Inspectors, or Weight Enforcement Officers) , weight enforcement officers have exclusive authority to enforce this subchapter in any area of this state, including all ports of entry between Texas and the United Mexican States.

(b) If a noncommissioned employee weighs a vehicle under Section 621.402 (Weighing Loaded Vehicle) and determines that an enforcement action, such as the issuance of a citation, is warranted, the employee may take enforcement action only if the employee is under the supervision of an officer of the Department of Public Safety.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 621. Gen. Provisions Relating to Vehicle Size & Weight
##### § 621.408 — Powers of Weight Enforcement Officers

(a) Except for the authority granted to a port-of-entry supervisor or inspector by Section 621.409 (Weighing of Loaded Vehicles by Port-of-entry Supervisors, Inspectors, or Weight Enforcement Officers) , weight enforcement officers have exclusive authority to enforce this subchapter in any area of this state, including all ports of entry between Texas and the United Mexican States.

(b) If a noncommissioned employee weighs a vehicle under Section 621.402 (Weighing Loaded Vehicle) and determines that an enforcement action, such as the issuance of a citation, is warranted, the employee may take enforcement action only if the employee is under the supervision of an officer of the Department of Public Safety.

*Acts 2005, 79th Leg., Ch. 313 (S.B.619), Sec. 3, eff. September 1, 2005.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: Except for the authority granted to a port-of-entry supervisor or inspector by Section 621.409 (Weighing of Loaded Vehicles by Port-of-entry Supervisors, Inspectors, or Weight Enforcement Officers) , weight enforcement officers have exclusive authority to enforce this subchapter in any area of this state, including all ports of entry between Texas and the United Mexican States.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: If a noncommissioned employee weighs a vehicle under Section 621.402 (Weighing Loaded Vehicle) and determines that an enforcement action, such as the issuance of a citation, is warranted, the employee may take enforcement action only if the employee is under the supervision of an officer of the Department of Public Safety.
